programme.title: Surviving Love
programme.desc: A couple decides to settle their divorce in a civilized manner without lawyers, which brings complications.

programme.title: Rush of Fear
programme.desc: Rosanna Arquette and Chris Potter star as couple Alex and Jack, who are away on a romantic weekend to try to salvage their broken marriage. Their trip turns into a nightmare when Jack is kidnapped.

programme.title: One Love
programme.desc: A rasta musician meets a gospel singer when they both enter a music contest in Kingston Jamaic. They fall for each other but are kept apart by the Girl's father the Pastor, who wants her to marry into the church.

programme.title: Love Again
programme.desc: Amidst a distant relationship with his father and losing his girlfriend to his best friend, Nathan's brokenness leads him on a journey of rediscovering hope and a healing heart to love again.

programme.title: Dragon Fury
programme.desc: When two vloggers are mysteriously burned to death, a team of experts must venture into an abandoned radiation zone to find a deadly dragon.

programme.title: American Yakuza 2: Back to Back
programme.desc: The violent impulses of Bob Malone (Michael Rooker), a former Los Angeles policeman forced to leave his job in disgrace, get the better of him during a bank robbery, and he wipes out nearly all the robbers. Meanwhile, Koji (Ryo Ishibashi), a Yakuza killer in town to meet with the local Mafia, turns the tables on a deranged terrorist who threatens to bomb an eatery. Corrupt cops bring Koji in, but he soon escapes from custody -- taking Malone and his daughter, Chelsea (Danielle Harris), hostage.

programme.title: Limitless
programme.desc: Facing unemployment and his girlfriend's rejection, writer Eddie Morra is sure that he has no future. That all changes when an old friend gives him a drug that produces enhanced mental acuity. Stoked on the untested chemical, Eddie rises to the top of the financial world and attracts the attention of a tycoon (Robert De Niro) who intends to use him to make a fortune. But terrible side-effects and a dwindling supply threaten to collapse Eddie's house of cards.

programme.title: Point Blank
programme.desc: Mickey Rourke and Danny Trejo star in this action-packed drama about a gang of psychotic criminals who break out of prison and take control of a Texas shopping mall.

programme.title: Le café de l'amour
programme.desc: La propriétaire d'un café où tout le monde tombe amoureux risque de tout perdre lorsque la grande demande d'une célébrité tombe à plat.

programme.title: L'homme de ma vie et moi
programme.desc: Nikki demande à son voisin de se faire passer pour son copain le temps d'une fin de semaine où elle devra se mêler à l'élite littéraire.

programme.title: Un mariage sur mesure
programme.desc: Will Millington arrive dans la boutique de robes de mariée d'Hannah McKenzie avec l'intention de renouveler la marque.

programme.title: Un rendez-vous presque parfait
programme.desc: Winnie est une jeune écrivaine qui tient un blogue sur lequel elle parle de sa vie amoureuse. Elle y raconte un premier rendez-vous.

programme.title: All The King's Men
programme.desc: A dynamic backwoods personality rides a wave of populist fervor straight into the governor's mansion.

programme.title: Born Free
programme.desc: The true story of Joy Adamson and her husband, George, who adopt a slain lioness' cubs.

programme.title: It Happened One Night
programme.desc: A heiress falls in love with the newspaperman she enlists to help her escape her father.

programme.title: The Bridge on the River Kwai
programme.desc: Allied commandos are dispatched inside the Burmese jungle to blow up a bridge built by British POWs.

programme.title: aTypical Wednesday
programme.desc: A well-intentioned man's weekly therapy visit takes a turn when a kid is "left behind" at the therapist's office and has to find his way home.

programme.title: Baby Done
programme.desc: A would-be adventurer panics when she gets pregnant by her long-term boyfriend, a man who couldn't be happier at the prospect of fatherhood.

programme.title: Babysplitters
programme.desc: Two couples who are ambivalent about having children decide to share one baby. It seems like the perfect compromise until things spiral out of control.

programme.title: Bittersweet Symphony
programme.desc: A musician finds her life getting complicated as she finishes her first Hollywood soundtrack.

programme.title: Eat Flowers
programme.desc: Photographer Cig Harvey's best friend Mary is diagnosed with a rare form of leukemia.

programme.title: Century Sisters
programme.desc: Betty and Annamarie Donovan, sisters for 94 years, roommates for 40, now it's Annamarie's 109th birthday.

programme.title: Imillaskate: The Cholita Skaters of Bolivia
programme.desc: In Bolivia, a collective of Indigenous women challenge gender stereotypes through skateboarding.

programme.title: Je Suis Elodie
programme.desc: Elodie's work and personal lives collide when she gets a breast cancer diagnosis.

programme.title: My Dearest Artsakh
programme.desc: Distanced from her home, displaced Armenian teenager Knar grieves her time in Artsakh (Nagorno-Karabakh).

programme.title: Apolonia, Apolonia
programme.desc: The film is a coming-of-age story of a girl, who finds her place in the art world.

programme.title: Je Suis Elodie
programme.desc: Elodie's work and personal lives collide when she gets a breast cancer diagnosis.

programme.title: Girls Section

programme.title: Heavy Hitters
programme.desc: It's payback time when Lou, Matt, and Froy stake out the baseball bus that got away and this time, they've got a foolproof plan. Lou, Matt, and Froy head to a late-night construction site to repossess an enormous crane. And, Sonia and Froy face their worst nightmare when a gang of bikers follows them back to the office.

programme.title: Matt Vs. Sonia
programme.desc: First, Sonia and Froy respond to a tip from an informant and wind up in the middle of an epic story of intrigue, betrayal, and drunk girls fighting each other. Then, a high-class croquet party gets interrupted when Matt and Lou come through to repossess a Volkswagen And finally, it's a trip to a parking garage to repossess a stolen limo - with a gaggle of partiers in the back. Will Matt and Sonia be able to hook and book, or will they wind up at each others' throats?

programme.title: Disaster at the Derby
programme.desc: The team gets back to work, but have their anger management sessions had any effect? Sonia and Lyndah find themselves surrounded by a gang of roller derby girls who've got the skills to protect their car, Lou and Sonia get lost in translation when they're forced to contend with a pair of Russian movers and their yuppie lawyer clients, and Matt, Lou, and Froy are in a battle of the scams when they try to trick a group of con artists into giving up their vehicle.

programme.title: T-Bone Trouble
programme.desc: The team's work with anger management starts to pay off - with disastrous consequences. Matt and Sonia get into a standoff with a wealthy divorcee, his daughter and her raging boyfriend.

programme.title: The Junior Partner
programme.desc: Now that Lou has accepted Matt's offer to buy into the company, the team is back in action, but there are still some unanswered questions. Sonia, Lyndah, and Froy interrupt a rich kid's brunch party to take away his ride, Matt and Lyndah attempt to repo an unusual vehicle from a man who thinks he's Uncle Sam, and Lou and Matt are in a losing match with an unruly gang of jacked-up rugby players.

programme.title: Daddy's Little Girl
programme.desc: The destruction of the repo team's truck has left the future of the company in the balance, but Matt's got a trick up his sleeve that might keep them afloat. Lou and Sonia head to a dark alley to take a van away from a pair of libidinous teens with a dark secret, Sonia and Froy come face to face with the world's most ornery baker and come away a little worse for wear, and Matt and Froy get in way over their heads when they attempt a repo at a high-octane truck racing track.

programme.title: The Cop, the Pizza Delivery Man, and the Repo Men
programme.desc: First, Lou sends Sonia and Froy out to repo from a pizza delivery man way behind on his payments. Then, Matt and Froy deal with an unhelpful citizen when they have to repossess from a cop. And finally, Sonia, Lyndah, and Matt deal with another repossessor when trying to repo a repo truck, until Lou can get on scene and offer the debtor a deal.

programme.title: Now Serving Mrs. Pizarro
programme.desc: First, Matt and Froy discuss what they will do with their severance pay once the company is sold, but have to put discussion on hold while they repo repo a hovercraft golf cart from a snooty golfer. Next, Sonia and Ronnie's easy day at the salon gets disastrous when the should-be debtor serves Sonia with a lawsuit. And finally, Froy and Carlos head out to a worksite to repo the bulldozer that Carlos should have been training for.

programme.title: Exterminate or Retaliate
programme.desc: First, Lou calls in an exterminator and has Froy, Sonia, and Lyndah go to an address to ambush them for their truck. Next, Matt, Froy, and Lou bring the debtors to the ground when they head out to repo a back hoe tractor. And finally, Froy, Lou, and Lyndah bully their way through a bunch of ignorant punk kids to grab some four wheelers.

programme.title: Tactical Planning
programme.desc: Matt and Froy walk into a moral gray area when they repossess a vehicle by stealing it. Then, Sonia and Matt discover their target van is being used in an investigation of highly toxic material. And, Matt poses as a valet to retrieve a car from an irate diner and his befuddled date.

programme.title: Blow-Up and Cool Down
programme.desc: Lou and Lyndah reclaim a riding mower from a housewife and her grab-happy husband. Then, Sonia and Matt head out on a scorcher of an afternoon for a repossession. Will they be able to beat both the heat and a pair of geriatric debtors? And, Sonia and Lyndah are left without words when they see the dark and embarrassing secret held in the backseat of a debtor's unpaid vehicle.

programme.title: Froy Gets Beat Up
programme.desc: Sonia and Lou see the signs of the bad economy first-hand when a rich couple willing give up their late-model Landcruiser. Then, Matt and Sonia need to contend with a spoiled princess whose daddy forgot to pay the bill on her Jetta.

programme.title: Ruffians and Wrestlers
programme.desc: Sonia and Matt have an unpleasant confrontation with a group of skateboarding ruffians who don't want to give up their car. Froy and Sonia try to take a vehicle from the world's most volatile couple. Plus, Lou and Froy wind up in unknown territory when they take an order to repossess from a squad of wannabe backyard wrestlers.

programme.title: Native Operation
programme.desc: First, Matt and Lou confront an ungentlemanly drunk at an upscale gentleman's club in an effort to repossess his late model BMW. Then, Sonia and Matt head over to the wrong side of the tracks to reclaim a motorcycle from a different kind of biker. And finally, Matt and Froy cross the border into a nearby reservation to wrangle with a belligerent debtor and two unimpressed representatives of the tribal police.

programme.title: The Showstopper
programme.desc: While attempting a repo of a "green energy" bio-diesel vehicle, Froy's (Froylan Tercero) lack of knowledge of the vehicle's specialized fuel system sets the bus ablaze... and when his error causes it to explode, he and Matt (Matt Burch) abandon builders/owners Mickie (Michael Q. Schmidt) and Joey (Gino Calabro) in the middle of the desert.

programme.title: The Swing Clinic: Firestone 1 of 2
programme.desc: In this episode of The Swing Clinic, Jimmy Hanlin visits the Kaulig Championship at Firestone Country Club in Akron, Ohio. He sits down with Kaulig Companies Executive Chairman Matt Kaulig to discuss his ties to the event and community. On the course, Hanlin works with Kaulig Racing's AJ Allmendinger, Ty Dillon, and Daniel Dye, offering practical swing tips with plenty of laughs. PGA Tour pro Dicky Pride also joins to share driver distance techniques, focusing on patience and smooth transitions. Mixing celebrity golf, instruction, and local flavor, the show captures the excitement of a major tournament week.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Land of the Giants: Idaho Mule Deer Part 2
programme.desc: Steve's quest for a giant mule deer buck continues in central Idaho. To help Steve in his journey, Ryan Callaghan has offered up one of his best spots as well as his keen spotting skills. The duo backpack hunt in some of the West's steepest and prettiest mountains seeking the colossal buck of Steve's dreams. In part two Steve finds the buck he's been searching for his whole life. The dream is realized and Steve and Ryan feast on a slow cooked roast.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Cooking Special: Butchering a Whole Deer
programme.desc: In this Cooking Special, Steve demonstrates how to butcher a whole whitetail deer.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Duren Deer Camp: Wisconsin Whitetail With Helen and Brittany Part 1
programme.desc: Helen Cho and Brittany Brothers are back for a double episode. This time around they're hunting whitetail deer in Wisconsin on the famed Duren farm with Doug Duren and Steve as their guides. Arriving a few days early to scout and tune up their shooting with some squirrel hunting, the ladies enter the next chapter of their hunting journey.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Duren Deer Camp: Wisconsin Whitetail With Helen and Brittany Part 2
programme.desc: Helen Cho and Brittany Brothers are back for a double episode.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Steve Makes the Team: Kentucky Small Game
programme.desc: Kevin Murphy and his band of Kentucky small game hunters introduce Steve to the finest Kentucky has to offer. Gray squirrels with tree hounds and cottontails with beagles, in Kentucky, it's the dogs that do the hunting. Cathead biscuits, sawmill gravy, and fried small game round out the Kentucky adventure.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: A Trip of Firsts: Mexico Coues Deer
programme.desc: Steve and Janis head into the remote mountains of Sonora, Mexico, to pursue the elusive Coues deer.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: Steven Rinella heads to the historic beaver trapping grounds of Wyoming to explore the legacy of the mountain man era. As he sets traps along the Green River, Steve immerses himself in the methods and stories of early American trappers. Alongside his quest to catch beavers, he delves into the fascinating history of how beavers shaped the fur trade and American frontier life. It's a journey through both the rugged landscapes and the rich heritage of the American West.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and ecologist Karl Malcolm head to the Cibola National Forest, guided by a century-old tip from conservationist Aldo Leopold, to hunt Merriam's wild turkeys. Their adventure showcases the forest's rich history and the legacy of Leopold's conservation efforts while they track these elusive birds through challenging terrain.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Cooking Special: Smoked Meats With Eduardo Garcia
programme.desc: Steven Rinella learns new culinary tricks from Chef Eduardo Garcia, a wild game enthusiast from Charged Film & Montana Mex. As Eduardo shares his expertise in smoking meats and preparing wild game dishes, Steve takes notes, eager to expand his culinary repertoire and bring fresh flavors to his own wild game cooking adventures.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: Steven Rinella joins ranch manager and wildlife biologist Ben Binnion in Texas to explore the often-overlooked aspects of hog management. During the annual pig roundup, Steve learns about the ecological impact and challenges of controlling the hog population, providing a deeper understanding of the complexities of hunting and wildlife management in the Lone Star State.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: Steven Rinella heads deep into Montana's rugged mountains to hunt black bears, reflecting on two decades of memories in this harsh yet captivating landscape. Joined by memories of his brother and their youthful adventures, Steve navigates grizzly country, contemplates the passage of time, and explores the evolving nature of hunting. Through encounters with both bears and the terrain, Steve seeks not just game but a connection to the past and the lessons it holds.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Nevada Mule Deer with Joe Rogan
programme.desc: Steven Rinella teams up with Joe Rogan in Nevada's high desert to bowhunt for elusive mule deer bucks. Surrounded by vast public lands and rugged terrain, they navigate the challenges of close-range stalking with their bows. With Joe's enthusiasm for archery and Steve's experience, the duo pushes their limits in pursuit of these wary deer, embracing the highs and lows of hunting in one of America's most unforgiving landscapes.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: Steve and Janis are deep in the wilderness of eastern Alaska on a challenging moose and caribou hunt. Joined by Janis' father, Papa Jani, who wants one last adventure before age catches up, they endure the highs and lows of remote hunting. With patience wearing thin and the weather turning, the group pushes through thick willows and foggy mornings, hoping for a bit of luck and a successful hunt before their journey's end.

programme.title: Tom Clark
programme.desc: Tom Clark was born and raised, in that order. Clark's mix of well-crafted jokes and spontaneous crowd work has led to appearances on TBS's ‘Conan,' CBS's ‘Late Late Show, and Comedy Central's ‘Premium Blend'. Clark has traveled coast to coast doing stand up and has performed internationally as part of New Faces at the prestigious‘Just For Laughs Comedy Festival' in Montreal and on The Main Stage at ‘The Capetown Comedy Festival' in South Africa. Clark is also an actor and has appeared on such shows as NBC's ‘Outsourced', TNT's ‘The Closer',and the Hallmark made for TV movie ‘The Wish List'. After landing the role on ‘Outsourced,' director Ken Kwapis invited Clark to be in the Drew Barrymore movie, ‘Big Miracle'. You can also see Clark in Columbia Pictures ‘Freaks of Nature.'

programme.title: Drew Allen
programme.desc: Cursed with the ability to over-analyze even the most basic of human interactions, comedian Drew Allen has been delighting audiences with his distinct brand of anxious comedy since 2010. Covering everything from the perils of modern relationships to the existential dread of grocery shopping, Drew's comedy examines both the big things and the little things through an insightful, hilarious lens. In addition to performing stand-up comedy in Salt Lake City for the last 3 years, Drew has also written sketch comedy for BYUtv's Studio C and is currently the president of Brigham Young University's stand-up comedy club, Humor U. If you're looking to laugh until it hurts while also maybe working through a few personal issues, look no further.

programme.title: Doug Thompson
programme.desc: With a different look on the everyday life, and the ability to find positive funny analogies on the pitfalls of it all comedian Doug Thompson is a ray of light. He became an opening act for "The Impractical Jokers" from Tru Tv hit show and has never look

programme.title: Collin Moulton
programme.desc: Collin is one of the most fun, personable, and sought after headliners today. He has had his own stand-up special on SHOWTIME, he's been on NICKELODEON, and was recently featured on LAST CALL WITH CARSON DALY. Touring for over 15 years Collin has gained an ease and enjoyment on stage that makes every show worth watching.America knows and loves him. His familiarity and energy engages and relieves the audience before he even speaks. His electric storytelling style makes you care about every topic he explores. Collin's comedy is the release needed from our every day lives. Come needing to laugh tonight, leave wanting to laugh forever!Collin Moulton

programme.title: Adam Cozens
programme.desc: A hilarious performer, Adam has been seen on IFC's “The Birthday Boys,” HBO's “Got No Game,” and TBS's “Lopez Tonight.” His stand up has been featured on Nickelodeon's “NickMom,” Hulu's “West Coast Funnies” “Tell Your Friends: The Concert Film,” “The CoExist Comedy Tour” documentary, “ComedyTime,” as well as heard worldwide on Sirius|XM Radio.

programme.title: Andrew Rivers
programme.desc: Andrew's very personal and fun storytelling comedy, combined with his exemplary work ethic has earned him high praise from some of the best comedians in the business. Andrew is a featured writer, actor, and comedian on the TV show “UpLate NW.” Andrew has been featured on NBC, FOX, and the Bob & Tom Radio Show and is a regular opening act for Christopher Titus. Andrew was also featured on the popular Buzzfeed web series “Truth or Drink“, has performed in over 35 states and his new podcast called “Don't Quit Your Night Job” was featured in the Seattle Times.

programme.title: Kevin Downey Jr.
programme.desc: New York City comedian and Las Vegas regular, Kevin Downey Jr. covers a wide variety of topics from reading a book on how to be a better comedian and why he doesn't drink anymore.

programme.title: Ken Rogerson
programme.desc: Chicago comedian Ken Rogerson jokes about pulling muscles watching tv, skydiving, and why everyone should skip instead of walk.

programme.title: Mike Guido
programme.desc: Mike Guido has made his mark as a nationally known headlining comedian. He has made eight national TV appearances including SHOWTIME, A&E, MTV, and VH1 and has worked with some of today's biggest stars such as Jay Leno, Robin Williams, and Ellen Degenneres.

programme.title: Jenna Kim Jones
programme.desc: Jenna Kim Jones is a comedian known for her strong personality and little to no upper body strength. She recently came to grips with the fact that she will never be able to do a pull up and earn her 4th grade Presidential Fitness Award though she plans to exploit this limitation into a book or reality television deal. Jenna was born in Seoul, Korea and lived there for approximately 8 years until she moved to Provo, Utah. At 18, Jenna left Provo to attend New York University where she majored in Producing and Writing for Television. During her time at NYU, she interned at Follow Productions, The Martha Show, The Late Show with David Letterman, The Daily Show with Jon Stewart and CBS Primetime Casting. After graduating from NYU, Jenna began performing stand up comedy while she worked at The Daily Show with Jon Stewart. After working at The Daily Show for almost 4 years, Jenna met her husband Allan Moss and the two were married in July of 2012 at which point Jenna left New York City to move to Los Angeles, California to pursue comedy (and sunshine). When she isn't working on her Netflix addiction, Jenna spends her days and nights writing and telling jokes all over the country. Once upon a time, Huffington Post named Jenna One of 18 Funny Women You Should Be Following On Twitter. For those of you who don't know, that is a compliment. However, it's when she gets her name on a Buzzfeed list that she'll know she's really made it. Jenna has appeared on The Daily Show with Jon Stewart and performed stand up alongside some of the best comedians including Jim Gaffigan, John Oliver, Wyatt Cenac and Aasif Mandvi. Her podcast, #SorryNotSorry with Jenna Kim Jones, is one of the Top 200 Comedy Podcasts on iTunes. Her first stand up comedy special also #SorryNotSorry with Jenna Kim Jones is available for sale at www.jennakimjones.com/shop. JKJ released a 2nd Comedy Special, “Jenna Kim Jones Live In Hollywood” as part of the “Everyone Laughs” series on Sirius XM Laugh USA in September of 2016. Her comedy plays regularly on both Sirius XM channels Laugh USA and Jeff and Larry's Comedy Roundup. Jenna is also known for her work as the on camera host/narrator of the feature length documentary, “Meet The Mormons” which was released in theaters nationwide on October 10, 2014 and is, as of now, the 31st most successful documentary of all time.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Journey to the classroom with your favorite Baby Einstein friends! programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Hop aboard to see vehicles that travel by land, water and sky! See real-life images of cars, boats and planes in this on-the-go adventure that features cameos from some of your favorite Baby Einstein characters, all while listening to music from around the world.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby Einstein: Let's Learn about Dogs, Cats and More
programme.desc: Meet Cats, Dogs, Birds, Bugs and Woodland Creatures! Learn simple words through narration and repetition. Meet all sorts of creatures from domestic kittens to jungle cats, puppies, big and small birds, butterflies, lady bugs, otters and deer!! See whimsical toys and puppets.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby Da Vinci: From Head to Toe
programme.desc: From the day their eyes open, babies begin to observe new things around the world and about themselves. Baby Da Vinci From Head to Toe presents them with a playful and interactive introduction to the human body in three languages -- English, Spanish, and French. With a whimsical blend of adorable puppets, real children and animals and famous works of art, this captivating program also features upbeat classical music.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Isn't the world exciting?! Learn about things found in nature like flowers, grass, trees, and leaves with real-life footage and narration. Sing-along to popular nursery rhymes and learn about common bedtime themes. Teach baby how to say words like eat, milk, and all done verbally and through sign language. Discover words and activities about playtime and friendship.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Baby Mozart is an introduction to Mozart's beautiful music, which has been proven to have positive affects on people of all ages, especially babies. Images of brightly colored toys and known objects aid in the audio presentation of child-friendly arrangements and sound effects.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Within a baby's first year of life, new experiences can transform what might otherwise seem to be ordinary events into exciting opportunities for imaginative play. Baby Neptune exposes little ones to the wonders of water in their world -- whether they're stomping in the rain, splashing in the bathtub, playing "catch me if you can" with the tide on the beach, or "meeting" the fascinating creatures that live in rivers, lakes and oceans.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby Einstein: Let's Learn About Space and Sky
programme.desc: Sing along to your favorite classical melodies and enjoy engaging imagery in this space and sky themed episode. Young viewers can learn words like star and moon through repetition and narration, explore with the Baby Einstein characters, or simply dance along.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Meet fascinating creatures from across the globe and learn to run, hop, and crawl like your favorite animal friends. Dance and sing along to discover animal names and the native habitats of the world's most unique creatures. See real-life images of lions and zebras on the African savannah, then learn animal names through repetition and narration, or simply watch your favorite animals play!

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Your little one will want to keep a beat, snap his fingers and march his feet! Join new characters Pillie, the musical woodpecker, and Coco, the dancing cockatoo, and share the excitement of rhythm with your baby. World of Rhythm explores rhythm in music, rhythm in nature and rhythm as a form of expression.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: My First Signs: See and Sign With Baby
programme.desc: As babies grow, their eye-hand coordination begins to improve as their ability to interact with their surroundings blossoms. It is the perfect time for My First Signs, A playful introduction to 20 common words and phrases from baby's world -- including ''mommy'', ''daddy'' and ''I love you'' -- both Spoken and in Sign Language.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: With the Dolittle, and other Baby programs under their belts, the tots who follow Julie Aigner-Clark's Baby Einstein series will sidle up to Baby Newton expecting a similar exploration of building blocks to brilliance, but this time there's a wrinkle or two. Animation makes its first appearance, and parents steeped in keep-it-simple culture may object to the springy "I Know My Shapes" song, a departure from classical-only video predecessors.

The girls turn to her for direction and she points them where she thinks the berry might be.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake is planning a first birthday party for her sister Apple Dumpling, so she sets off on an adventure to find the perfect party supplies.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Disappearing Dog Show
programme.desc: When Huckleberry and Blueberry's Patti Persimmon-style mystery story is rejected by an online mystery magazine, Blueberry and Huck have to come up with a new way to raise support for Huckleberry's Hotel for Homeless Pets back in Berry Big City. Strawberry suggests they hold a big dog show! Plum makes over-ambitious plans to display talents that her dog does not quite have, trying to teach him complicated dance routines.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Berry Bitty City is holding its first city-wide talent show! Hearing that it's the music students of superstar Cherry Jam, a talent scout from Big Bitty City plans to show up. Strawberry and her friends excitedly anticipate her arrival.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Apple isn't paying attention when Strawberry describes their campout tradition of telling "Tall Tales." So Apple tells her thrilling adventure of a wild road rally she entered, only to surprise the girls by proving the story is actually true.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Apple's rave review on her berryweb page of Strawberry's new dessert starts a booming mail order dessert business for Berry Bitty City. When Strawberry's stuffed-up nose makes her bake a bad-tasting cake, Apple, afraid the truth will hurt Strawberry's reputation, proclaims it's delicious. The fib snowballs when the Queen of Berryvania reads Apple's review and orders Strawberry's new cake for her ball.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Sour teasing Apple escalates into a "prank war" between the two of them. After the girls' feud disrupts the town, they are sentenced in a Berry Bitty City-style trial to camp alone together for a whole weekend. Miraculously, they emerge best buds, and their friends are relieved, until Sour's influence on Apple turns her into a mini-Sour, and they have to do a personality "makeover" on Apple.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Rarity asks Applejack to be a judge in a fashion show.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.sub-title: Bad Thing No. 3 Part 1 / Bad Thing No. 3 Part 2
programme.desc: Rarity tries to get rid of her bad luck. / Rarity receives some much-needed advice from an unexpected source.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.sub-title: Pinkie Pie: Hyper Helper Part 1 / Pinkie Pie: Hyper Helper Part 2
programme.desc: Pinkie Pie prepares for the final round of the Royal Jelly Juggernaut. / Pinkie Pie enters the final round.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Starlight Glimmer is sent by the Cutie Map to solve a friendship problem.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: An avalanche falls on Yakyakistan.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Discord invites Fluttershy to his realm for tea.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Applejack, Apple Bloom, and Big McIntosh learn about their parents' love story.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Twilight Sparkle publishes her and her friends' friendship journal.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.sub-title: Princess Probz Part 1 / Princess Probz Part 2
programme.desc: The ponies help Pinkie Pie in an audition. / Pinkie Pie has to audition all on her own.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.sub-title: The Best of the Worst Part 1 / The Best of the Worst Part 2
programme.desc: Rainbow Dash's competitive spirit is put to the test. / Rainbow Dash faces off against Dishwater Slog.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Spike accidentally invites Ember and Thorax to Ponyville on the same day.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Applejack, Rarity, and Rainbow Dash tell their favorite legends after their camping trip is ruined.

programme.title: Blippi's Great Green Adventure
programme.desc: In this special episode, Blippi celebrates Earth Day with his Great Green Adventure. Caring for the environment is one of our prime responsibilities and Blippi teaches children how by taking them on a puzzle-solving adventure.

programme.title: Starlight, Star Bright
programme.desc: With time running out before the Grand Opening celebration for the new Marketplace, Strawberry's friends get the idea to invite her favorite singer, superstar CHERRY JAM, to be the guest singer. The girls are blown away when their berryjuice cell phone gets a text message that Cherry accepts their invitation! Strawberry is touched by their surprise, but reminds them that now the town and Marketplace really have to shine! The girls and Berrykins rush to turn the Marketplace Café into a suitable performance area for a big star like Cherry Jam.

programme.title: Practice Makes Perfect
programme.desc: Cherry Jam is so enjoying staying in Berry Bitty City, that she calls her managers in Big Bitty City and informs them that she is going to stay for a much needed vacation! Strawberry and the girls are thrilled that she will be with them for a little longer, and they do all they can to make their guest comfortable. But Cherry doesn't want to be treated like a guest she wants to fit in and contribute to the community. Plum suggests that she teach music lessons Plum has always wanted to learn the drums, as it would enhance her ability to give dance lessons with live rhythmic accompaniment.

programme.title: Link into the VRAINS
programme.desc: n Den City, duels are held in the highly advanced VR world Link VRAINS, which is always under attack by the Knights of Hanoi. However, a hero named Playmaker has arrived to face the Knights, capturing a program they want called an Ignis.

programme.title: Seize the Wind!
programme.desc: Playmaker continues his duel with the Knight of Hanoi using the new Speed Duel rules. Playmaker is backed into a corner by the Knight's Cracking Dragon, but a data storm soon envelopes the field and Playmaker activates his Skill.

programme.title: Blippi's Summer Games
programme.desc: Blippi is back with Blippi's Sports Summer Games Movie For Kids!

programme.title: Top Talent
programme.desc: Berry Bitty City is holding its first city-wide talent show! Hearing that it's the music students of superstar Cherry Jam, a talent scout from Big Bitty City plans to show up. Strawberry and her friends excitedly anticipate her arrival. Strawberry and Cherry are going to sing a duet, Orange is going to juggle, Plum will perform a ballet, Blueberry is writing and directing a play that the Berrykins will perform, Lemon will design animal-shaped hairstyles and display them herself, Princess Berrykin is going to yodel, and Berrykin Bloom will to do a stand-up comedy routine. Raspberry, who was the big star of the music recital (with only nine mistakes), is going to perform her same keyboard piece.

programme.title: A Star is Fashioned
programme.desc: Raspberry is discovered by a talent scout who wants her to move to Berry Big City and design a new line of Glitter fashions! She will be provided with her own studio, staff, publicity, commitments from big department stores—everything she needs to be a big fashion designing star! All her friends are thrilled for her. Only Cherry is a bit less enthusiastic about Raspberry's stardom, which Raspberry mistakenly interprets as jealousy. During Raspberry's preliminary trip to the city to begin arrangements for her new design studio, her friends get a taste of life without her. They miss her, and it makes them sad. Cherry admits to Strawberry that she's concerned for Raspberry—stardom is not all it's cracked up to be. We see that Cherry was worried about her friend, not jealous. Raspberry returns to pack up her little boutique. Her friends barely recognize her.

programme.title: Blippi's Alphabet and Transportation Adventures
programme.desc: Join Blippi on a series of exciting educational journeys where he uncovers the Case of the Missing ABCs, explores the charming Ranch at the Pier, and delves into London's rich transportation history at both the Transport Museum and the RAF Museum. From solving puzzles and meeting farm animals to discovering vintage buses and historic airplanes, Blippi makes learning an adventure filled with curiosity and fun.

programme.title: Blippi's Fun and Colorful Exploration
programme.desc: Get ready for a vibrant and entertaining escapade with Blippi as he dives into a world of colors at Billy Beez, creates a whimsical pizza in the kitchen, transforms into a playful pirate on a treasure hunt, and explores the fascinating world of dinosaurs at the Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History. Each adventure is packed with laughter, creativity, and educational moments, making learning a joyful experience for kids.

programme.title: The Berry Big Harvest
programme.desc: Finding she has some free time one morning, Strawberry visits Berrykin Bloom who is filled with excitement over his new crop of Berry Bitty fruits and vegetables his garden has never grown so much produce! He can't wait to share his new crop (the perfect bitty size) with Berry Bitty City, so Strawberry volunteers to help him transport the produce to Orange's General Store. Soon Orange's store is overflowing with Berry Bitty Fruits & Veggies. It's the berry first time Orange has not been able to find a “place for everything” in her store.

programme.title: Room at the Top
programme.desc: Strawberry is really touched when her girlfriends and the Berrykins volunteer to help her with the construction of her new marketplace, so when the girls suggest they take charge of designing Strawberry's dream-bedroom to go on the second story, of course Strawberry agrees. While construction is going on, the girls each show Strawberry their individual ideas for the bedroom. Grateful for the support and not wanting to dampen anyone's enthusiasm, Strawberry tells each of them yes (actually, more like not-no).

programme.title: Don't Forget the Leaves!
programme.desc: Sid and his friends investigate and discover that leaves are food for animals, they provide shade, and they help make nutrients so flowers and trees can grow!

programme.title: Leo, The Wildlife Ranger
programme.desc: The Junior Rangers help a mob of Meerkats find a new home.

programme.title: Leo, The Wildlife Ranger

programme.title: Wow Wow Wubbzy
programme.desc: Wow Wow Wubbzy - Escape From Dino Island: Wubbzy and his friends travel to Dino Island to get a close look at the dinosaurs living there. Also, Widget's invention doesn't work, so she gets the blooey blues.

programme.title: Care Beare Care A Lot
programme.desc: Care Beare Care A Lot - Ice Creamed / Heatwave: Grumpy rebuilds Amigo Bear's ice cream cart so it provides an endless supply of ice cream. / Grizzle is at it again, using a giant "Magnifrier" to heat up Care-a-lot!

programme.title: Care Beare Care A Lot
programme.desc: Care Beare Care A Lot - Erased / Unbearable: Grizzle's eraser ray doesn't work, but it did turn the Care Bears' badges upside down and changed the way they act! / The Care Bears give Funshine a trophy to say "thank you", but it goes to his head and gets him and his friends trapped in Grizzle's lair!

programme.title: Care Beare Care A Lot
programme.desc: Care Beare Care A Lot - Care-Ful Bear / A Case Of The Grumpies: Funshine feels responsible for Share getting hurt during a game, so he dons a cape to become Care-ful Bear, the champion of safety. / Grumpy is storming around Care-a-lot and seems grumpier than usual, so Cheer decides to "un-grump" the Grumpster.

programme.title: Rainbow Rangers
programme.desc: Rainbow Rangers - Monkey Business / Lost Panda Cub: Rosie, Lavender and Pepper journey to the Amazon to save a marmoset that Preston Paxton has captured as a pet for his daughter, Patty. The Rangers have to help a lost panda cub get home.

programme.title: Rainbow Rangers
programme.desc: Rainbow Rangers - A Whale of a Problem: The Rangers rescue a beached whale who has been tormented by sonar waves. The Rangers have to stop a bee colony from collapse.

programme.title: Angry Birds
programme.desc: Angry Birds - Bearded Ambition & More: Fun adventure episodes from Angry Birds, Piggy Tales and Stella! In this episode watch "Bearded Ambition", "Cold Justice" and more!

programme.title: Angry Birds
programme.desc: Angry Birds - Eating Out & More: Fun adventure episodes from Angry Birds, Piggy Tales and Stella! In this episode watch "Eating Out", "The Great Eggscape" and more!

programme.title: Talking Tom and Friends
programme.desc: A wealthy opportunity arises when a hotshot billionaire gets stranded in Talking Tom's garage.

programme.title: Talking Tom and Friends
programme.desc: The battle to win a geeky magazine contest brings out the worst in everyone.

programme.title: Atomic Betty
programme.desc: Atomic Betty - The Good, The Bad, and Sparky: On Earth, Betty gets salesmanship advice from her father as she tries to sell candy door-to-door. Meanwhile, in space, Sparky becomes a movie star when famous galactic director Antoine Lucci casts him in his latest movie, but when Maximus gets involved, the danger on the set becomes very real!/ Betty's mom buys a new automatic feeding and grooming machine for Purrsy, and she wants Betty to put it together! Betty takes the machine with her to space, where her mission is to test out a new, technologically advanced spaceship, complete with its own new, advanced robot, X-8.5. Will X-5 be replaced by this new, technologically advanced robot?

programme.title: Atomic Betty
programme.desc: Atomic Betty - The Great Race / Poached Egg: Betty has a date after school with Dylan, the new boy in her class, but first she must stop Maximus, who has created a new explosive device known as the Shredder, which upon detonation, with destroy the time/space continuum. Now Betty is in a race to defuse the Shredder, save the galaxy and return to Earth in time for her date! / Chef Bernadette quits as Maximus' personal chef, so Maximus orders Minimus to make him an omelette Piraxian Rhino Egg. Problem is, there is only one such egg left in existence! Betty and her crew must stop Maximus from poaching the last egg. Meanwhile, on Earth, Betty is blamed for starting a food fight in the school cafeteria. Will Noah's video evidence clear her name?

programme.title: Shaq's Garage
programme.desc: Shaq's Garage - The Big Trail / Big Bad Car: Shaq gets a call from his farmer friend to help rescue three baby cows that have escaped out of their corral. Boone is joined by a reluctant Himari and Bates, who learn how rewarding getting outside of their comfort zone can be. / The Shaq Paq watches a classic horror movie. Himari is scared but is too stubborn to admit it. With a little good-natured trickery, and a lot of support, the rest of the Paq help Himari admit the truth and face her fears.

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: General Havoc reinforces Divatox helping her capture the Turbo Megazord.

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: With no way to defend Angel Grove, the Rangers turn to the Phantom Ranger for assistance.

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: General Havoc and Divatox set a trap for the Phantom Ranger in order to capture his powerful Phantom Ruby.

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: The Turbo Rangers race to stop the Turbo Megazord and save the Phantom Ranger as his life force ebbs away.

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: T.J. tries to help Bulk and Skull find out who is raiding Angel Grove for spare parts.

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: The Turbo Rangers charge into their final battle.

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: The remaining Power Rangers blast off into outer space to try and save Zordon.

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: Divatox returns to Earth seeking vengeance against the Power Rangers Turbo.

programme.title: Power Rangers

programme.title: Power Rangers

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: Divatox's Chromite steals the Turbo Rangers' morphing energy to create evil shadow Rangers.

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: Divatox sends Numbor to cause Angel Grove to sink into the ocean while Kat worries about her weight.

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: The Blue Senturion time travels to present-day Angel Grove with a message from the future for Dimitria.

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: Divatox sends Electrovolt to Angel Grove to ruin Adam's soccer team.

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: The Rangers prepare for their final assignment while Divatox decides to capture Tommy.

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: Tommy, Kat, Adam, and Tanya pass their powers on to a new generation of Power Rangers.

programme.title: Power Rangers
programme.desc: Two intelligent cars from outer space called Lightning Cruiser and Storm Blaster travel to Earth.

programme.title: Everything's Changed
programme.desc: The dream chaser's jobs are on the line now that the dream world is under the guardianship of a mysterious new hero called “Dreamsmasher.”

programme.title: Holiday Gift Swap Disaster
programme.desc: While the rest of the group gets creative and makes new, personalized gifts from scratch. In the end, it's a reminder that it's not about the gifts—it's about the thought and effort you put into showing you care.

programme.title: Day Of The Great Devourer
programme.desc: Disaster threatens as the ninja must fight to defeat the all consuming Great Devourer in a desperate attempt to save Ninjago.

programme.title: LEGO Marvel Super Heroes: Avengers Reassembled
programme.desc: Finally, the Avengers confront Ultron and his Ultron Minions as they attack Avengers' Tower. Ultron's ultimate plan: use all the data/stuff they've stolen to free the newly rebuilt IRON LEGION, destroy the Avengers, and control the world. Tony dons the hastily repaired Hulk Buster Armor and it's on! It's a huge battle as the Avengers try to save their beloved HQ and defeat Ultron. Will they succeed? And, more importantly, can their party be saved after Ultron tries to take down their Tower?The Avengers must now fight one of their own, and they're not sure what to do! Cap and Thor lead the charge, but can't fend off Iron Man and Ultron's Minions. Meanwhile, Black Widow, Hawkeye and Hulk track down a signal that seems to be controlling Iron Man from a castle in the “Little Sokovia” section of New York. There they fight HYDRA and BARON VON STRUCKER and learn that YELLOWJACKET is the “bug” that infected Iron Man's suit for Ultron. To help break Iron Man free from Ultron's control, the Avengers call in their own bug...ANT MAN!The Avengers prepare for a party with a surprise guest when Ultron crashes the preparations by using mind control on Iron Man. While trying to rescue their leader, the rest of the team discovers that Baron Stucker, HYDRA, and Yellowjacket are in on Ultron's plans. While preparing for a party at Avengers' Tower, THE AVENGERS (IRON MAN, CAPTAIN AMERICA, BLACK WIDOW, HAWKEYE, THOR, HULK and VISION) notice Iron Man acting strangely. When Iron Man volunteers to go out and get more ice, they find it odd: when has Tony every volunteered to get anything? Suddenly, ULTRON appears on a screen: he's taken control of Iron Man! To make matters worse, Ultron takes over all the versions of Iron Man's armor and they fly off!

programme.title: The Book Of Obsession
programme.desc: The Knights are tasked with protecting the King's valuable Ned Knights comic book during the Bookingheim Knight-a-Con. But with all the costumed kids there, will they recognize the real Jestro and Book of Monsters coming to get them? King Halbert instructs the Knights that they must take his valuable Ned Knights comic book to the “Knight-a-Kon” in Bookingham. It will be the first be first prize at the Knight-a-Kon. But the Book of Monsters wants the book, too. Having devoured Ned Knightly comic books in it's down time it has become fanatically obsessed with the storyline. General Magmar suggests they simply attack the place and take what they want, but Jestro tells them they can't: everyone will be in costume and they'll be thousands of comics there. This calls for a clever “infiltration” mission (which will also allow Jestro to see the Kon, which he's always wanted to do.) So Jestro goes into the Kon with the BOM and some monsters. And it's easy because everyone is in costume Jestro, the BOM and their monsters fit right in at the convention and Clay and crew have their work cut out for them trying to separate fiend from fan. Macy keeps going on-and-on about the story in the King's comic book as the Knights keep a watchful eye on it. Robin even shows up in the “Minitrex” – a small version of the Fortrex he built just for the Knight-a-Kon. He's convinced he can win the costume competition that is the big closing event for the Kon. At the end of the Kon there's a big awards ceremony for “Best Costume.” The prize: “Ned Knightly, Knight of Knighton, Issue #1.” The BOM must get it and scheme to get closer to it during the costume contest. He's just…about…to get the book when…the impatient Magmar attacks! Everyone thinks it's part of the big show! “Ned Knightly, Knight of Knighton, Issue #1” is tossed from person-to-person-to-person in the melee. The Knights must do something, but they're too far away from the Fortrex to download any NEXO-Power. Robin pulls up in his Minitrex they can use it to get NEXO-Power! Robin wins the best costume for his Minitrex and wins the Ned Knightly Comic which he then gives to Macy…she deserves to read it.

programme.title: Anita Oday, Göran Engdahl, Roman Dylag & John Poole
programme.desc: Anita O'Day boasts two wonderful concerts from 1963 and 1970 that present the “jazz singer supreme” in impeccable form. O'Day's horn-based approach to singing is in full effect throughout both shows including stand-out renditions in each show of audience favorites “Tea For Two” and “Sweet Georgia Brown” (both reprised from her triumphant appearance at the 1958 Newport Jazz Festival.) This show is a fitting testimony to one of jazz music's true originals and shows unequivocally why she is ranked in the top tier of vocalists along with Ella Fitzgerald, Sarah Vaughan, and Billie Holiday.

programme.title: Lalah Hathaway
programme.desc: Winner of five Grammys in four consecutive years, including the Grammy for best R&B album in 2017, Lalah Hathaway is a force impossible to ignore. The American artist has shared the scene with Prince, Snoop Dogg, Stevie Wonder, Anita Baker, Mary J. Blige, Frank Ocean and many others, an irrefutable proof of her keen musical sense and enormous talent. With a 26-year career, Hathaway is still highly sought after by the biggest names in music. See her transform the 2018 Sing Jazz festival scene into a real R&B club, where she flaunts her voice and stage presence out of the ordinary.

programme.title: Ron Carter
programme.desc: Ron Carter, Bobby Hutcherson and Joe Chambers are legends of Blue Note Records. They have established themselves as forward-thinking and inventive band leaders, composers and studio musicians, astonishing audiences around the world with their creativity and technical versatility.

programme.title: Earl Hines
programme.desc: Despite his advanced age, the legendary jazz pianist Earl Hines was at the top of his game when he performed in the Belgian town of Spa in 1977. With a band consisting of Rudy Rutherford (reeds), Jimmy Leary (bass), and Eddie Graham (drums), Hines livens up the room with his interpretations of “I Can't Believe That You're In Love With Me” and “The Man I Love.” Marva Josie joins the group for soulful renditions of “A Sunday Kind of Love” and the well-known classic “Kansas City” before the set comes to a close with “Caravan,” which features a lengthy drum solo by Graham.

programme.title: Sarah Vaughan, Richard Davis, Ronnell Bright & Art Morgan
programme.desc: Sarah Vaughan: Live in '58 & 64 features the Divine One in her prime, wrapping her sultry voice around jazz standards such as “Lover Man”, “Misty”, and “I Got Rhythm” and popular showtunes such as “Over The Rainbow” and “Maria”. One of the greatest voices of the 20th Century, her renditions of songs by Harold Arlen, Leonard Bernstein, Johnny Burke, the Gershwins, and Stephen Sondheim are pure diva magic. These three performances demonstrate why Sarah Vaughan is invariably mentioned in the same breath as Ella Fitzgerald and Billie Holiday.

programme.title: Bill Evans
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Jazzmeia Horn
programme.desc: Vocalist Jazzmeia Horn's 2019 Malta Jazz Festival appearance was a celebration of jazz's future, infused with the soul of its past. Featuring Keith Brown (piano), Rashaan Carter (bass), Irwin Hall (flute and saxophone), and Anwar Marshall (drums), the young singer's performance was a reminder of jazz's enduring power to speak to the heart. Situated at the Valletta waterfront outside Our Lady of Liesse Church, Horn's concert was a truly spellbinding experience.

programme.title: Charlie Parker
programme.desc: The documentary The Story of Jazz is a seamless array of performances, commentary, and compelling historic insights about jazz, zooming in on the cross-cultural influences that produced this constantly evolving and enduring music. Many call jazz the greatest art form of the 20th century. This absorbing weave of sounds, rare film clips, stills and interviews shows how this truly American expression spread to become a universal, musical language, from the New Orleans traditions, stride, swing, boogie woogie, big band, jump band, blues, bebop, Afro-Cuban, cool, and free-form, to jazz vocalists and dancers… Never before have the filmed comments of so many important great jazz artists been assembled for one project, and never before has the history of jazz been told as vividly and with such attention to historic detail.

programme.title: Soloists of the Lucerne Festival Orchestra, Lucas Macías Navarro, Stefan Dohr & Jonathan Wegloop
programme.desc: The soloists from the Lucerne Festival Orchestra present two septets in this engaging 2020 concert, recorded at Lucerne Culture and Congress Center (KKL), Switzerland. It opens with Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's Divertimento No. 11 in D major, K. 251, known as the ‘Nannerl-Septet'. Mozart composed it in 1776, probably on the occasion of his sister Maria Anna's name day, whose nickname was ‘Nannerl'. The septet is scored for oboe, two horns, two violins, viola and double bass. The program continues with Ludwig van Beethoven's Septet in E-flat major, Op. 20, which is scored for clarinet, horn, bassoon, violin, viola, cello and double bass. This 1799 piece achieved great popularity during Beethoven's lifetime. It was arranged for many various instrumental ensembles, including versions for two guitars, and piano four-hands, but also a trio for clarinet (or violin), cello, and piano, arranged by Beethoven himself.

programme.title: Gewandhausorchester Leipzig
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Les Dissonances & David Grimal
programme.sub-title: Schubert - String Quartet No. 15 in G major
programme.desc: Les Dissonances is a collective of artists founded by violinist David Grimal in 2004. The conductorless ensemble consists of musicians from the most prestigious European orchestras, international soloists, and young talents. In this performance, Les Dissonances' string quartet – consisting of David Grimal (violin), Hans Peter Hofmann (violin), David Gaillard (viola), and Xavier Phillips (cello) – performs Franz Schubert's String Quartet No. 15 in G major, D. 887, Op. 161. Schubert composed this work, his final string quartet, in a mere ten days in June 1826. However, this highly original piece, characterized by its restless shifts between major and minor, was not published until after Schubert's death in 1851. This performance was recorded at Cité de la Musique in Paris, France, in 2014.

programme.title: Luca Vignali, Angelo De Angelis, Carmine Pinto & Eliseo Smordoni
programme.sub-title: Beethoven - Quintet for piano and winds, Op. 16
programme.desc: Luca Vignali (oboe), Angelo De Angelis (clarinet), Carmine Pinto (horn), Eliseo Smordoni (bassoon), and Linda Di Carlo (piano) perform Ludwig van Beethoven's Quintet for piano and winds in E-flat major, Op. 16. The piece, completed in 1796, is assumed to have been modelled on Mozart's Quintet for piano and winds, K. 452. Both quintets are scored for the same unusual instrument combination, are in the same key, and feature the same three-movement structure. A marked difference is that Beethoven, as a piano virtuoso, wrote the piano parts to highlight the strengths of his playing. Besides, the composer regularly places this instrument in the center of attention. Beethoven published the work in 1801 in two versions: as a quintet for piano and winds, and as a quartet for piano and strings (also designated Op. 16). This performance was recorded at Perugia's Auditorium Santa Cecilia, Italy, on June 23, 2022.

programme.title: Herbert Blomstedt & Gewandhausorchester Leipzig
programme.sub-title: Symphonies in D by Mozart and Voríšek
programme.desc: Conductor Herbert Blomstedt and the Gewandhausorchester take us on a musical journey to the Czech Republic in this 2020 concert from Leipzig's Gewandhaus. The program opens with Bohemian composer Jan Václav Hugo Voríšek's Symphony in D major, Op. 23 (1821). Although the composition – Voríšek's only symphony – was never performed during the composer's lifetime, it has become one of his most-performed works today. Also on the program is Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's Symphony No. 38 in D major, K. 504. This work, which premiered while Mozart was visiting Prague in 1787, is often referred to as the ‘Prague Symphony'.

programme.title: Orquesta Filamonica de Buenos Aires, Carlos Gari, Daniel Barenboim & Mora Godoy
programme.sub-title: A Tango Night - Live from Buenos Aires
programme.desc: 2006 closed with a spectacular festival of Argentinean music broadcast live from Buenos Aires. At the height of the Argentinean summer, the Orquesta Filarmónica del Teatro Colon under Daniel Barenboim (conductor & soloist) join bandoneon virtuoso Leopoldo Federico and his Orquesta Tipica for an extraordinary New Year's Eve show with popular tangos and Latin American orchestra classics. The old master of tango, José Carli, created enchanting new arrangements of works by Argentinean artists Astor Piazzolla, Carlos Gardel, Julio de Caro, Alberto Ginastera and Horacio Salgán. Performances by leading tango dancers Mora Godoy and Junior Cervilla from Buenos Aires add atmosphere and round off the night.

programme.title: Cream
programme.desc: Fronted by Eric Clapton, Cream was the prototypical power trio, playing a mix of blues, rock and psychedelia while focusing on chunky riffs and fiery guitar solos. In a mere three years, the band sold 15 million records, played to SRO crowds throughout the U.S. and Europe, and redefined the instrumentalist's role in rock.

programme.title: Elvis Costello
programme.desc: British singer/songwriter Elvis Costello extended the lyrical range of punk and new wave music with songs like "Alison," "Everyday I Write the Book" and "Veronica." Leading music critics cast fresh light on the career of the Eagles.

programme.desc: Born to Die is the second album by singer-songwriter Lana Del Rey, and the first to be officially released on January 27, 2012 and that would lead to global success In countries such as the United Kingdom the album was the best selling of that year, that recognition made her a guest of honor in one of the most popular London festivals iTunes Festival, where she would open the awe of all attendees on the night of September 25 2012 at the Roundhouse in the city of London.

programme.title: Oasis
programme.desc: Filmed at Wembley Arena, London, this concert film will give all fans a spectacular Live experience from start to finish. Classic Oasis featuring unforgettable version of their hits: 'Champagne Supernova', 'Don't Look Back In Anger', 'Rock N Roll Star' and many more.

programme.title: The Grateful Dead
programme.desc: Grateful Dead, mixed genres such as psychadelia, blues, folk, country, rock 'n' roll and jazz to create their incredibly unique rock sound. Known for changing set lists for each show, and for sometimes playing for more than four hours in one set, the Dead created songs like "Sugar Magnolia," "Casey Jones" and "Scarlet Begonias." While the group toured with various musicians until it disbanded in the late 1990s. Leading music critics cast fresh light on the career of Grateful Dead.

programme.desc: Enjoy these Clown Fish with Music and Tranquil Ocean Sounds any time of day! Perfect for Relaxing, Studying, Sleeping, Meditating, Date Night, Dinner Parties and More.

programme.title: Flying Over Oahu
programme.desc: "Flying Over Oahu" offers a 97-minute aerial journey over Oahu's iconic coastlines with nature sounds and music by Alex Smith (The Cynic Project). From Waikiki Beach to Turtle Bay, enjoy breathtaking 4K landscapes, ending with a stunning Waikiki sunset.

programme.title: Flying Over Spanish Islands
programme.desc: In their first collaboration, cinematographer Stefan Zimmerman and Nature Relaxation™ producer David Huting present "Above the Spanish Islands." This 50-minute 4K aerial film captures Mallorca and the Canary Islands, set to ambient music by Diamans, Electus, and Michael St. Laurent.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland: Part I" provides a soothing aerial journey through eastern Iceland's fjords and valleys, starting at sunrise in Brei_dalsvik. Enjoy views of mists, a waterfall, and the Brei_dals' river, set to nature sounds and music by The Cynic Project, showcasing East Iceland's beauty.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland 2
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland II: Summer in the Golden Circle" presents a serene aerial view of Iceland's south coast, starting at Skogafoss and moving to Vik, with black sand beaches, cliffs, and Vatnajökull glacier. Filmed in 2020, this 1-hour film features local wildlife and immersive music.

programme.title: Flying Over Kauai
programme.desc: "Flying Over Kauai" provides a soothing aerial journey over Hawaii's Garden Island, featuring the lush NaPali Coast, waterfalls, canyons, and beaches. Paired with new music by The Cynic Project and nature sounds, this 4K film offers a visually and sonically immersive experience.

programme.title: The Soothing Sun
programme.desc: Rejoice and relax in the splendor of the glorious Sun for a full hour, with this new video by David Huting. This version features only the sounds of Nature, and offers a visual journey featuring many scenes of glorious sunrises and sunsets from locations such as Yosemite, Big Sur, Hawaii, Santorini, Sydney, The Canadian Rockies, Arches, Canyonlands, the Fiji Islands, and much more. Featuring music by Relax Moods.

programme.title: Rugged Beach: Hawaii
programme.desc: A new day dawns on Hawaii's Pa'ako beach as waves crash on the shore and the sky brightens with gorgeous hues.

programme.title: A Day in Santorini
programme.desc: Turn any screen into a window the the Greek Islands with this immensely relaxing HD visual journey through the unique and beautiful coastal village of Oia, found on the far tip of Santorini Island. Slowly awaken your senses as the morning sun rises over the sea, and watch as day slowly passes into dusk. Distant waves break and birds chirp adding an a nice ambient music that combines to create a video that's so relaxing it's almost trance-inducing.

programme.title: Rocky Mountain Wonders
programme.desc: Discover fall in the Rockies. The lush forests and snow-covered mountaintops offer glimpses of pure wilderness.

programme.title: Saltbox Houses: Newfoundland
programme.desc: Take a breather on Newfoundland and Labrador's picturesque Change Islands, famous for its brightly-hued saltbox houses.

programme.title: Rainbow Reef Relaxation II 2
programme.desc: Immerse your senses on an unparalleled underwater journey to some of the world's most vibrant coral reefs and undersea paradises as seen in true 4K Ultra High Definition, paired to soothing new music by Relax Moods. Viewers will discover a plethora of stunning sea creatures, thanks to the incredible underwater cinematography by Peter Schneider filmed over the last several years.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland
programme.desc: Flying Over Iceland: Part I is a stunning yet soothing aerial journey to the remote fjiords and valleys of eastern Iceland. The first of a series of 1 hour films, Part I begins at sunrise on a perfect summer day in Breiðdalsvík, where a golden sun lights up the majestic valley. After soaring through the golden sea mists and offering a glimpse of the stunning Breiðdalur Valley (its name means ‘Wide Valley'), viewers then head inland to a hidden, magical arched waterfall for a refreshing immersive flight that begins at the base of the falls and reaches above the clouds. Next, as the morning turns into a sunlit afternoon, viewers are swept inland to the crystal clear waters of the the impressive Breiðdalsá river, to cool off in some falls along with a pair of local swans. Well known for salmon fishing, the turquoise blue river winds its way scenically across the valley basin to the sea, which is where the film ends - but not before a chance encounter with a local reindeer! The final flight in Part 1 offers a sunny afternoon look at the tiny village of Breiðdalsvík from afar and offers amazing blue water vistas of with green islands, seagulls, a rock island that that looks like a cat, a scenic lighthouse, and more.
